actual_current_surface_status: bounded-support / O_sp/O_H literature bridge; no current-surface closure import
exact_next_action: If taking the literature route, implement a same-surface FMS-inspired O_H operator certificate and use the existing source-Higgs builder/postprocessor with GEVP-style pole-residue rows.  Otherwise continue chunk production.
methodology_shape: Future positive attempt should build a same-surface gauge-invariant canonical-Higgs operator certificate, then measure a C_ss/C_sH/C_HH correlator matrix with GEVP or isolated-pole residue analysis.
proposal_allowed_reason: The literature suggests a future FMS-style gauge-invariant O_H operator plus GEVP/Gram-pole extraction path, but it does not supply a same-surface Cl(3)/Z3 O_sp=O_H theorem or measurement.

source_rows.pr230_boundary: Does not identify the PR230 scalar source O_s or O_sp with the required gauge-invariant Higgs operator.
source_rows.route_action: Use as design context for a future same-surface O_H certificate, not as proof input.
source_rows.title: Analytical relations for the bound state spectrum of gauge theories with a Brout-Englert-Higgs mechanism
source_rows.useful_shape: FMS-style gauge-invariant composite Higgs operators can be related to gauge-fixed Higgs objects after the gauge-Higgs system is supplied.

source_rows.pr230_boundary: Pole-structure matching in an EW gauge-Higgs theory does not supply PR230 C_sH/C_HH rows or O_sp/O_H overlap.
source_rows.route_action: If pursued, implement a gauge-invariant O_H candidate and validate it through the O_H certificate and Gram-purity gates.
source_rows.title: Gauge-invariant description of the Higgs resonance and its phenomenological implications
source_rows.useful_shape: A gauge-invariant bound-state Higgs description can share pole structure with the elementary Higgs in the FMS expansion.

source_rows.pr230_boundary: This is external lattice methodology and has no authority to define the Cl(3)/Z3 source coordinate as canonical Higgs.
source_rows.route_action: Use as methodology context for future EW/Higgs lattice rows, not as a retained closure import.
source_rows.title: Weak and Higgs physics from the lattice
source_rows.useful_shape: Recent lattice Higgs work treats weak/Higgs physics through gauge-invariant FMS-connected observables.

source_rows.pr230_boundary: GEVP supplies an extraction method only after a valid O_H operator family and C_sH/C_HH rows exist.
source_rows.route_action: Future source-Higgs production should prefer an operator matrix/GEVP pole-residue analysis over one-row assertions.
source_rows.title: On the generalized eigenvalue method for energies and matrix elements in lattice field theory
source_rows.useful_shape: Operator correlation matrices and GEVP analysis are standard tools for isolating states and matrix elements.

source_rows.pr230_boundary: It justifies the source-response measurement pattern, not the source-to-canonical-Higgs identity.
source_rows.route_action: Keep FH/LSZ chunks as production support; do not promote source-only response to physical y_t.
source_rows.title: Feynman-Hellmann approach to transition matrix elements and quasi-degenerate energy states
source_rows.useful_shape: Adding a perturbing operator to the action and reading energy shifts is a standard lattice route to matrix elements.

source_rows.pr230_boundary: Gauge independence of a pole is not a scalar-source overlap theorem and does not certify O_H.
source_rows.route_action: Use Nielsen/BRST material only as a guardrail for future EW pole definitions.
source_rows.title: On the gauge independence of the fermion pole mass
source_rows.useful_shape: Nielsen-identity methods can protect pole-mass statements under gauge variation in appropriate settings.

strict_non_claims: does not claim retained or proposed_retained y_t closure
does not import external literature as PR230 proof authority
does not identify O_sp with O_H
does not treat FMS pole-structure statements as source-Higgs Gram purity
does not use H_unit, yt_ward_identity, observed targets, alpha_LM, plaquette, or u0
